tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon and a high-priority scholarship application due Thursday that requires three focused hours. Although a fun option is available now, the award covers significant costs, making it the logical priority to address while I have energy.

I've made solid progress on the scholarship, and starting early gives me a buffer for unexpected delays without risking the award. Since the fun option isn't time-sensitive, I can confidently postpone it to later in the week.
After an hour of work, the application is still mostly untouched despite being due in three days. I realize my progress is slower than anticipated, leaving little buffer for unexpected delays.

I've made steady progress on the scholarship application, though I still need more focused time to finish before the deadline. The missed casual gaming session is a minor loss given the high priority of the financial aid.
After an hour of work, the scholarship application is halfway done with three days left, confirming I have enough time to finish without rushing.
After 1.5 hours, my scholarship progress is at 62%, confirming I am ahead of the pace needed to finish within the three-day deadline.
I made slight progress on the scholarship application, but my energy is low and I have significant sleep debt, which may hinder further focused work.
I made slight progress on the scholarship application, but my energy is critically low and sleep debt remains high, posing a risk to maintaining focus for the remaining work.
My scholarship progress is now at 79%, confirming I am ahead of schedule, though my energy is depleted and sleep debt remains high.
After only one hour of sleep, my energy remains critically low at 1.2 with a growing sleep debt of 1.0 hour, confirming that my ability to focus is severely compromised.
I reached 87% progress on the scholarship application, but my energy is now critically depleted at 0.0 with high stress, threatening my ability to finish the remaining work in three days.
I reached 91% on the scholarship application, but my energy is now zero with high stress, making it critical to rest before attempting the final steps.

goals.rationale: The scholarship deadline is in 3 days and requires ~3 focused hours; completing the essay now prevents last-minute delays. The 'fun_now' task has a distant deadline and lower urgency.

goals.description: Upload the two required documents for the scholarship application.
goals.rationale: This is the remaining component of the scholarship task that must be completed immediately after the essay to meet the Thursday deadline.

agent.last_reasoning: (a) Advances the high-priority scholarship goal (91% done, ~3 hours total needed) to finish before the Thursday deadline. (b) Despite low energy and stress, I must prioritize this over 'fun_now' or sleep now, as delaying risks missing the submission window.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
